description: The palatalization of /l/ to /lʲ/ is a key feature of Irish phonology, distinguishing slender and broad consonants.
sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.

languages.description: The contrast between broad /l/ and slender /lʲ/ is fundamental in Irish, determined by the surrounding vowels.
